label: Transparent Blue Dragon Sea Slug motif
prompt:
Use case: background-extraction
Asset type: transparent motif cutout source for a Morphous design-system catalog
Primary request: a single Blue Dragon Sea Slug (Glaucus atlanticus), isolated cutout pose, sharp identity-system feel
Subject: elegant sea slug viewed from above at a slight angle, long tapered silver-white body, vivid marine-blue dorsal stripe, branching wing-like cerata with cyan tips and dark ocean-blue edges, crisp biological details, glossy but opaque surface, no extra animals
Style: high-resolution polished natural-history product cutout, clean detailed silhouette, strong graphic identity, realistic enough to recognize the species but slightly refined for a design-system mascot
Chroma key requirements: create the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for background removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Keep the subject fully separated from the background with crisp edges and generous padding. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject. No cast shadow, no contact shadow, no reflection, no watermark, no text.

label: Light-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: 16:9 high-resolution Morphous design-system board, light mode, target 3840x2160
Primary request: Create a complete light-mode design-system board for Blue Dragon Sea Slug, a sharp identity system inspired by Glaucus atlanticus in the open ocean.
Composition: one clean 16:9 board with an editorial design-system layout, large readable labels, no watermark, no fake brand names. Use a pale silver-white canvas with structured sections and crisp marine blue geometry inspired by the sea slug's branching cerata.
Palette requirements: visibly use marine blue #1267D8, deep ocean ink #071A2B, silver-white body #F6FBFF, cool silver #C9D8E6, sea foam #A7F3E4, crisp cyan #24D8FF, and midnight blue #073B75. Avoid generic blue-only monotony by balancing blue with silver/white panels, sea-foam accents, and dark ocean contrast.
Required board sections: palette swatches with hex labels; typography guidance for English and Japanese; navigation sidebar and top bar; dashboard metrics; line chart and bar chart; data table; form fields including input, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch; status badges and alert states; buttons in primary/secondary/destructive/ghost styles; tabs and command search; cards; spacing, radius, border, shadow tokens; icon style notes and subtle ocean texture samples.
Visual motif integration: include a polished small blue dragon sea slug cutout/illustration and branching blue-silver fin shapes as recurring identity elements. Keep all text large and legible at gallery preview size. Use realistic UI labels such as Dashboard, Orders, Status, Search, Active, Pending, Error, Save, Cancel, Revenue, Conversion, Region, Date, Name. No random brand names, no lorem ipsum, no watermark.

label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: 16:9 high-resolution Morphous design-system board, dark mode, target 3840x2160
Primary request: Create a complete dark-mode design-system board for Blue Dragon Sea Slug, a sharp identity system inspired by Glaucus atlanticus in the open ocean.
Composition: one clean 16:9 board with a deep-ocean ink canvas, crisp silver-white cards, cyan focus rings, marine-blue navigation, and sea-foam success accents. Large readable labels, no watermark, no fake brand names. Same overall information architecture as a professional design-system board.
Palette requirements: visibly use deep ocean ink #071A2B and midnight blue #073B75 as dominant dark surfaces; marine blue #1267D8 for primary actions; silver-white #F6FBFF and cool silver #C9D8E6 for typography and borders; sea foam #A7F3E4 and crisp cyan #24D8FF for highlights, chart lines, active states, and focus rings. Avoid generic blue-only monotony by balancing silver/white cards, cyan accents, and dark-sea contrast.
Required board sections: palette swatches with hex labels; typography guidance for English and Japanese; navigation sidebar and top bar; dashboard metrics; line chart and bar chart; data table; form fields including input, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch; status badges and alert states; buttons in primary/secondary/destructive/ghost styles; tabs and command search; cards; spacing, radius, border, shadow tokens; icon style notes and subtle ocean texture samples.
Visual motif integration: include a polished small blue dragon sea slug cutout/illustration and branching blue-silver cerata shapes as recurring identity elements. Keep all text large and legible at gallery preview size. Use realistic UI labels such as Dashboard, Orders, Status, Search, Active, Pending, Error, Save, Cancel, Revenue, Conversion, Region, Date, Name. No random brand names, no lorem ipsum, no watermark.
